mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and raises valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns turn positive over the next month when policy is easing. The signal is valid only when the rate cut is sustained rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and weaker future earnings, which pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment accelerates rather than remaining flat.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the hit rate remains below 40% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad final-demand strength that supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal requires both consumption and construction demand to improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production rebound after prior weakness signals improving real activity and margins, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect fails when production gains are entirely inflation-driven rather than volume-driven.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive after INDPRO rises for two straight months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPI inflation forces tighter policy expectations and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is active only when inflation accelerates enough to change the policy path.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% m/m and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-triggered episodes do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the combined CPI-FEDFUNDS signal is not stronger than CPI alone.

mechanism_narrative: A higher GDP level relative to the prior half-year indicates expanding aggregate demand and stronger earnings expectations, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The relationship fails when GDP gains come with a concurrent labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than half of quarters with rising GDPC1, or if the signal loses significance after accounting for recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth weakens while unemployment rises, the labor market is deteriorating fast enough to threaten earnings and risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month. The signal requires both softer hiring and higher joblessness together.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS weakness and UNRATE increases do not predict negative 20-day SPX returns better than either series alone, or if the conditional hit rate stays above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts collapse signals a demand downturn that feeds into construction, durables, and bank credit exposure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest after a clear break in housing activity rather than small fluctuations.
failure_condition: Reject if threshold breaches are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the thresholded signal has no incremental predictive value over a 3-year rolling window.
